title: Discord Integration
summary: Discord bot integration covering Developer Portal setup, privileged intents, per-user session isolation, slash command ACL via allow_admin_from, auto-threading, forum channels, media handling, history backfill, and mention control.
key_concepts: Per-user session isolation (each Discord user gets own session)
Slash command ACL via allow_admin_from role/user lists
Auto-threading: bot creates a thread for each conversation
Forum channel support as separate sessions
Media attachments: images forwarded to vision pipeline
Mention control: respond only when mentioned or in DM
RBAC via Discord roles mapped to command permissions

env_vars.name: DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Bot token from Discord Developer Portal

env_vars.name: DISCORD_GUILD_ID
env_vars.description: Guild ID to restrict bot to a single server; if unset bot works on all servers

env_vars.name: DISCORD_ALLOWED_CHANNELS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated channel IDs the bot will respond in

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Discord listener
Reset current Discord session conversation history

config_keys.description: List of channel IDs bot responds in
Automatically create threads for conversations (default: true)
Only respond when bot is @mentioned (default: false for DMs, true for channels)
Role names or user IDs that can use admin slash commands
Map of channel_id to system prompt override
Number of messages to backfill when resuming a thread (default: 20)

slash_commands.description: Reset the current session conversation history
Switch LLM model for current session (admin only)

code_examples.title: RBAC admin config
code_examples.code:
discord:
  token: ""
  allow_admin_from:
    - "Admin"        # role name
    - "Moderator"    # role name
    - "123456789"    # specific user ID

code_examples.title: Auto-threading disabled
code_examples.code:
discord:
  token: ""
  auto_thread: false
  mention_only: true

code_examples.title: Per-channel prompt
code_examples.code:
discord:
  per_channel_prompts:
    "9876543210987654321": "You are a coding assistant. Only discuss programming topics."

gotchas: MESSAGE_CONTENT privileged intent must be enabled in Developer Portal or the bot cannot read message text
Slash commands must be registered with Discord API; hermes gateway does this automatically on startup but may take up to 1 hour to propagate globally
Auto-threading creates a new thread per conversation which can clutter channels; disable with auto_thread: false
Discord rate limits apply: 5 requests/second per guild, 50/second globally
Bot needs Manage Threads permission to create and manage auto-threads
Forum channels require bot to have permission to create posts in the forum
History backfill re-reads thread messages on resume; large threads slow startup
Voice channel support requires PyNaCl and ffmpeg installed
full_text:
## Discord

Connect Hermes to Discord via the official bot API.

### Setup

1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) and create a new application.
2. Under **Bot**, create a bot user and copy the token.
3. Under **Bot → Privileged Gateway Intents**, enable:
   - **MESSAGE_CONTENT** (required to read messages)
   - **SERVER MEMBERS** (required for role-based ACL)
4. Under **OAuth2 → URL Generator**, select scopes  and , then the required bot permissions (Send Messages, Create Public Threads, Manage Threads, Read Message History, Add Reactions).
5. Invite the bot to your server using the generated URL.

### Minimal config

```yaml
discord:
  token: ""
  guild_id: 1234567890123456789
  allowed_channels:
    - 9876543210987654321
```

### Session model

Each Discord user gets their own isolated Hermes session. DMs and channel conversations are separate sessions. When  (default), each new channel conversation spawns a thread — subsequent messages in that thread continue the same session.

### Slash command ACL

Restrict admin commands (like ) to specific roles or users:

```yaml
discord:
  allow_admin_from:
    - "Admin"       # role name
    - "Moderator"   # role name
    - "123456789"   # specific user ID
```

### Auto-threading

By default, when a user sends a message in a channel (not a DM), Hermes creates a thread to keep conversations organized. Disable with:

```yaml
discord:
  auto_thread: false
```

### Forum channels

Forum channel posts are treated as individual sessions. Each post is a separate conversation with its own history.

### Mention control

In channels, the bot only responds when mentioned by default. In DMs it always responds. Override:

```yaml
discord:
  mention_only: false  # respond to all messages in allowed_channels
```

### Media and vision

Image attachments are automatically forwarded to the vision pipeline if the active model supports it. Audio attachments are transcribed via the configured STT provider.

### History backfill

When a user resumes a thread, Hermes backfills recent messages for context:

```yaml
discord:
  history_backfill: 20  # number of messages (default: 20)
```

### Per-channel prompts

```yaml
discord:
  per_channel_prompts:
    "9876543210987654321": "You are a coding assistant. Stay on topic."
```

### Voice channels

Hermes can join Discord voice channels and perform STT/TTS. Requires  and  installed. See [Voice Mode](voice-mode.md) for full voice channel setup.
